name: mansion-study
summary: A noble's private study — desk-dominant, book-lined, personal. Where the lord signs letters, hides ledgers, and thinks alone.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: squarish or slightly elongated toward the window

primary_palette.note: scholar-noble — desk faces the window, books wrap three walls
administrator / spymaster — the study plans the estate or the realm
intimate study — correspondence and fireside reading
standing scholar — no desk, a reading lectern and shelves

story_prompts: the lord has just stepped out — the letter on the desk is mid-sentence, candle still lit
ransacked — drawers pulled, books thrown down, a specific ledger is missing
the inheritor's study — the old lord's things untouched, the new lord's things stacked by the door
the study has a secret door behind a bookshelf and a second, smaller study beyond
an evening reading — fireplace bright, decanter open, nobody awake to finish the glass

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: focused warm (0.3-0.45) — desk candles, fireplace glow

anti_patterns: this is NOT a library — shelves line the walls but books are tools, not the point
desk is primary, NOT decorative — it dominates one axis of the room
avoid crowded bookshelves on every wall — leave room for a painting, a map, a window
